name: I got hurt at work in Murray Utah but didn't go to the doctor right away, can I still file a workers comp claim?
description: Yes, file within Utah's 1-year limit despite delay, but get medical records linking injury to work ASAP to avoid claim denial.[1]

mainEntity.name: I got hurt at work in Murray Utah but didn't go to the doctor right away, can I still file a workers comp claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, file within Utah's 1-year limit despite delay, but get medical records linking injury to work ASAP to avoid claim denial.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can still file a workers' compensation claim in Utah even if you delayed medical treatment, as long as you report the injury within 180 days and file within one year. Delays can weaken your case by making it harder to link injuries to the accident, but documentation from later visits helps establish causation.[1] Key factors include Utah's no-fault workers' comp system covering medical bills and lost wages regardless of fault, prompt employer notification requirement, and insurance carrier's right to choose your doctor. Report immediately, get treated soon, and avoid discussing details with anyone but your employer and doctor; consult a specialist to protect benefits. Time-sensitive rules make early legal advice essential.
